      Today

    1. Blue Owl has Scott Bessent on private credit watch

    2. The AI trade is punishing Big Tech. Here's why Apple has so far avoided the chaos.

    3. Why does Nvidia's stock trade at 'low' valuation levels? Our executive editor explores the reasons.

    4. The Dow sank over 800 points, and the Nasdaq and S&P 500 both dropped over 1%, as tariff and AI fears returned to Wall Street.

    5. The Dow led markets lower, sinking over 800 points amid Trump's shifting tariff threats and renewed AI fears.

    6. A hasty transition after the Supreme Court ruling has unleashed a fresh round of trade chaos. Here are the early winners and losers.

    7. Stocks open steady on Wall Street with focus on Trump's plan for tariffs

    8. Fed's Waller may no longer back rate cut if jobs report is strong

    9. ‘Money's moving out of tech’: Wall Street weighs stock market winners amid the AI scare trade

    10. Where’s my tax refund? Here's how to check your refund status.

    11. If you have less than $1,000 saved for retirement, you're not alone. Millions of Americans are in the same boat.

    12. In stagnant job market, worker sentiment 'is taking a hit'

    13. DHS to halt PreCheck and Global Entry programs amid shutdown (Bloomberg)

    14. Nvidia earnings, SCOTUS tariff fallout, geopolitical tensions rise: What to watch in the week ahead

    15. The tail end of 2025 saw a spike in borrowing as rising prices hit consumers hard, with new personal loans up 24%

    16. United Airlines escalates the credit card perk wars

    17. Nearly half of Americans have used AI to help with their personal finances

    18. Oil analysts say there's a supply glut — why it hasn't translated to lower prices this year

    19. After ordering an immediate 10% 'global' tariff rate, on Saturday President Trump said he was raising it to 15%

    20. We wrote about medical bills devouring Social Security checks. It struck a nerve.

    21. Trump signs 10% global tariff in bid to salvage trade agenda (Bloomberg)

    22. Sticky new inflation data expected to keep Fed on hold

    23. Bessent says tariff refund would be ‘ultimate corporate welfare’ (Bloomberg)

    24. The economy grew last year. Why aren't jobs catching up?

    25. 'It will not be automatic or immediate': Companies brace for a messy tariff refund process

    26. Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq jump to post weekly gains as Supreme Court strikes down Trump tariffs

    27. Trump blasted the Supreme Court's decision striking down his tariffs and said he's imposing a 10% 'global' tariff under a different authority

    28. Stocks perked up in the immediate aftermath of the Supreme Court's ruling striking down Trump's tariffs. The Nasdaq led gains.

    29. The Supreme Court ruled that Trump's most sweeping global tariffs are illegal, in a stinging rebuke of the president's signature economic policy

    30. US stocks slide after GDP cools, Fed-favored PCE inflation heats up

    31. The US economy grew at a far slower pace than expected to end 2025. President Trump blamed the government shutdown for the slowdown.

    32. Investors are bracing for a 'knee-jerk market reaction' when the Supreme Court hands down its verdict on Trump's tariffs. Here's what to expect

    33. Eric Trump shrugs off bitcoin's recent slump, takes aim at those who 'don't have the backbone'

    34. There's a new tug of war between Tesla bulls and bears

    35. The Dow led stocks lower as oil prices continued to rally amid increased tensions between the US and Iran

    36. Mortgage rates drop to lowest level in nearly 4 years

    37. Stocks fell at the open, as oil continued to rally amid escalating tensions between the US and Iran

    38. The US trade deficit widened in December and remained one of the biggest yearly deficits since 1960, amid a year of erratic tariff policy.

    39. Walmart posts modest quarterly earnings beat as new CEO John Furner takes the helm

    40. Meta's CEO testified as part of a landmark civil lawsuit accusing social media companies of building their platforms to get young users addicted

    41. The Trump administration slammed the New York Fed study that claimed that US consumers bear most of the costs of tariffs

    42. C.H. Robinson's CEO is fighting back against the great 'AI scare' of 2026

    43. Walmart is set to report earnings on Thursday morning. Here's what to expect

    44. The Nasdaq led gains on Wall Street as traders brushed aside AI worries for now. The Dow and S&P 500 rose for a third straight day.

    45. The central bank remains deeply divided over its rate path — with some members supporting further cuts, while others talked of a rate-hike scenario.

    46. For the first time in 20 years, Apple has become an outlier among tech giants. That has helped it avoid the AI ‘whack-a-mole’ gripping the market. (Bloomberg)

    47. US stocks rise at the open as AI worries recede, with Fed minutes ahead

    48. US stocks are off to their worst start versus the global market since 1995

    49. Cyber stocks are getting crushed by the 'AI scare trade.' That may create a buying opportunity for three stocks, analyst Dan Ives says.

    50. Ford is entering a new EV era

            Cavco Industries, Inc. (CVCO)

            • 1y Target Est 587.50

            Cavco Industries, Inc. designs, produces, and retails factory-built homes primarily in the United States. It operates in two segments, Factory-Built Housing and Financial Services. The company produces park model RVs; vacation cabins; and factory-built commercial structures, including apartment buildings, condominiums, hotels, workforce housing, schools, and housing for the United States military troops. It also produces various modular homes, which include single and multi-section ranch, split-level, and Cape Cod style homes, as well as two- and three-story homes, and multi-family units. In addition, the company provides conforming and non-conforming mortgages and home-only loans to purchasers of various brands of factory-built homes sold by company-owned retail stores, as well as various independent distributors, builders, communities, and developers. Further, it offers property and casualty insurance to owners of manufactured homes. The company distributes its products through a network of independent and company-owned retailers, planned community operators, and residential developers. The company was founded in 1965 and is headquartered in Phoenix, Arizona.

            www.cavcohomes.com
